title: lotus corniculatus subsp. ruprechtii
intro: Lotus corniculatus subsp. ruprechtii, also known as the meadow vetchling or narrow-leafed meadow vetchling, is a perennial herb belonging to the Fabaceae family and is native to Eastern Europe and Western Asia. It has been traditionally used in medicine due to its antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties, which help in treating various health issues such as skin conditions, wounds, and digestive problems. The plant's health benefits include its ability to reduce inflammation, promote wound healing, and exhibit antimicrobial properties. The herbal preparations of Lotus corniculatus subsp. ruprechtii include infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, which can be used to treat various health conditions, although further research is required to confirm its efficacy and safety.
